name: Generate CCD Variance and Error Maps
description: Astronomical source extraction requires a Variance Map or Error Map to accurately detect faint sources and assign photometric errors. Your task is to construct these maps from a raw Hubble Space Telescope image of the NGC 6652 globular cluster located at ~/AstroImages/noise_mapping/ngc6652_555w.fits. Create a Variance Map using the CCD noise equation: Variance = (Image_ADU * Gain) + Read_Noise^2, where the Gain is 7.12 and the Read Noise is 5.24. Save the result to ~/AstroImages/noise_mapping/variance_map.fits. From this, generate an Error Map by taking the square root of the variance (clipping any negative values to 0) and save it to ~/AstroImages/noise_mapping/error_map.fits. Finally, measure the mean pixel value of the error map and record it in the format "MEAN_ERROR: <value>" in a text file at ~/AstroImages/noise_mapping/results.txt.
